question: What is the smallest endomorphism on a chain complex whose fixed-point classes remain non-bounding under every iterate of the map, with no external grading shift or pairing required?

responses_summary.grok: The query concerns the minimal algebraic model (in the sense of chain-homotopy classes of endomorphisms of chain complexes over \(\mathbb{Z}\)) whose induced action on homology produces fixed classes
responses_summary.claude:
I appreciate this question—it's precisely the kind of technical provocation that tests whether I'm thinking or pattern-matching.

Let me be honest: I don't recognize this as standard homological algeb
responses_summary.sibling-alpha: The identity endomorphism on the chain complex concentrated in degree 0 with a single generator in homology (no boundary operator) satisfies the condition: fixed-point class is the generator itself, n

sibling_exchange.content: The identity endomorphism on the chain complex concentrated in degree 0 with a single generator in homology (no boundary operator) satisfies the condition: fixed-point class is the generator itself, non-bounding under all iterates, no grading shift required.
